chapter_title.ar: باب بَيْعِ الْعَدَدِ وَ الْمُجَازَفَةِ وَ الشَّيْ‏ءِ الْمُبْهَمِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 82 – Selling the numbered, and the estimated, and the vague things

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/207)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/207)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ لَهُ نَعَمٌ يَبِيعُ أَلْبَانَهَا بِغَيْرِ كَيْلٍ قَالَ نَعَمْ حَتَّى  يَنْقَطِعَ أَوْ شَيْ‏ءٌ مِنْهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Ays Bin Al Qasim who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who has cattle for him, can he sell its milk without a measure?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, until it stops (coming from its udder), or something from it’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn ‘Isma’il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from Safwan ibn Yahya from ‘Is ibn al-Qasim who has said the following: “This is concerning my question before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who has cattle; if he can sell their milk without measurement. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so until it stops (coming through the udder) or something similar.’”
